Fractionation-specific warning: Brain SRS concurrent = HIGH RISK (all vinca alkaloids); lung central/ultracentral SABR = HIGH RISK; lung peripheral SABR = HIGH RISK; liver/upper abdomen conventional = AVOID concurrent (especially vincristine, vinblastine); conventional lung/H&N/spine = MODERATE CONCERN; palliative bone RT = LOW CONCERN.
Timing-specific warning: Concurrent use usually acceptable if clinically indicated; verify PI/protocol
Medical Oncology discussion: Yes for brain SRS, SABR, or any high-dose concurrent RT
Senior RO review: Yes for brain SRS, lung SABR, liver RT
App warning / documentation text: Vinorelbine (vinca alkaloid): MODERATE CONCERN. HIGH RISK with brain SRS, lung SABR (peripheral or central), liver conventional RT. AVOID concurrent upper abdominal RT (hepatotoxicity). Senior RO review for SRS, SABR, or liver RT.

RT toxicity domains: Mucositis, pneumonitis, dermatitis, impaired healing, marrow suppression
Highest-concern RT sites / OARs: Lung (all RT, especially SABR), liver SABR, pelvis/bone SABR, H&N
Fractionation-specific warning: Lung RT (conventional radical or SABR) = HIGH RISK (everolimus/temsirolimus pneumonitis + radiation pneumonitis additive); lung SABR peripheral = HIGH RISK; lung SABR central/ultracentral = HIGH RISK; liver SABR = HIGH RISK (RILD + mTOR hepatotoxicity); pelvis/bone SABR = HIGH RISK; brain GBM conventional = MODERATE CONCERN; brain SRS = CAUTION.
Timing-specific warning: Consider hold interval for high-risk sites
Medical Oncology discussion: Mandatory for any thoracic RT or SABR
Senior RO review: Mandatory for any thoracic RT or SABR at any extracranial site
App warning / documentation text: Everolimus (mTOR inhibitor): HIGH-RISK with thoracic RT (all fractionations) and SABR at any site. mTOR-inhibitor pneumonitis + radiation pneumonitis are additive and potentially fatal. Senior RO + Med Onc review mandatory for any SABR or radical thoracic RT.
Verification status: Unverified first-pass inventory: reconcile against ARTG/PBS/eviQ before clinical release
